Kikuyu: Nguo ciake ikĩerũha cua; gũtirĩ mũndũ gũkũ thĩ ũngĩahotire gũcithambia cierũhe ũguo.
English: His clothing became glistening, exceedingly white, like snow, such as no launderer on earth can whiten them.

Kikuyu: Na marĩ o hau makiumĩrĩrwo nĩ Elija na Musa, makĩaranĩria na Jesũ.
English: Elijah and Moses appeared to them, and they were talking with Jesus.

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akĩĩra Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Rabii,* nĩtwagĩrĩirwo nĩ gũikara gũkũ. Reke twake ithũnũ ithatũ, kĩmwe gĩaku, na kĩmwe kĩa Musa, na kĩmwe kĩa Elija.”
English: Peter answered Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good for us to be here. Let’s make three tents: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”

Kikuyu: (Petero oigire ũguo tondũ nĩarigĩtwo nĩ ũrĩa ekuuga, nĩ tondũ wa ũrĩa maamakĩte.)
English: For he didn’t know what to say, for they were very afraid.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ hagĩũka itu, rĩkĩmahumbĩra, na mũgambo ũkiuma itu-inĩ rĩu, ũkiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩwe Mũrũ wakwa, ũrĩa nyendete. Mũiguagei!”
English: A cloud came, overshadowing them, and a voice came out of the cloud, “This is my beloved Son. Listen to him.”

Kikuyu: Na kahinda o kau makĩĩhũgũra, no mationire mũndũ ũngĩ arĩ hamwe nao, tiga o Jesũ.
English: Suddenly looking around, they saw no one with them any more, except Jesus only.

Kikuyu: Narĩrĩamaikũrũkagakuumakĩrĩma-inĩ, Jesũakĩmaathamatikeeremũndũonaũrĩkũũrĩamonete, nginya rĩrĩa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agaakorwo ariũkĩte akoima kũrĩ arĩa akuũ.
English: As they were coming down from the mountain, he commanded them that they should tell no one what things they had seen, until after the Son of Man had risen from the dead.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkira na ũhoro ũcio, no makooranagia o ene atĩrĩ, “kũriũka kuuma kũrĩ akuũ nĩ kuuga atĩa.”
English: They kept this saying to themselves, questioning what the “rising from the dead” meant.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga arutani a watho moige atĩ Elija no nginya ambe ooke?”
English: They asked him, saying, “Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ Elija no nginya ambe ooke ahaarĩrie maũndũ mothe. Ningĩ nĩ kĩĩ gĩgĩtũmĩte kwandĩkwo atĩ Mũrũ wa Mũndũ no nginya athĩĩnĩke mũno na aregwo nĩ andũ?
English: He said to them, “Elijah indeed comes first, and restores all things. How is it written about the Son of Man, that he should suffer many things and be despised?

Kikuyu: No ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, Elija nĩarĩkĩtie gũũka na andũ makamwĩka o ũrĩa wothe meendaga kũmwĩka, o ta ũrĩa ũhoro wake wandĩkĩtwo.”
English: But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they have also done to him whatever they wanted to, even as it is written about him.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa maakinyire harĩ arutwo arĩa angĩ, makĩona kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene kĩmarigiicĩirie, na arutani a watho magĩkararania nao.
English: Coming to the disciples, he saw a great multitude around them, and scribes questioning them.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa andũ othe moonire Jesũ, makĩgega mũno magĩtengʼera makamũgeithie.
English: Immediately all the multitude, when they saw him, were greatly amazed, and running to him, greeted him.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ mũrakararanĩria nao?”
English: He asked the scribes, “What are you asking them?”

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũmwe warĩ kĩrĩndĩ-inĩ kĩu akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, ngũkũreheire mũrũ wakwa, ũkoragwo arĩ na ngoma thũku na ndaaragia.
English: One of the multitude answered, “Teacher, I brought to you my son, who has a mute spirit;

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yothe yamũnyiita, ĩmũtungumanagia thĩ na akarutaga mũhũyũ na kanua, akahagarania magego na akomagara. Njũũririe arutwo aku maingate ngoma ĩyo thũku, no matinahota.”
English: and wherever it seizes him, it throws him down; and he foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and becomes rigid. I asked your disciples to cast it out, and they weren’t able.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ rũciaro rũrũ rũtetĩkagia, nĩ nginya rĩ ngũikarania na inyuĩ? Ngũmũkirĩrĩria nginya rĩ? Ndeherai kamwana kau.”
English: He answered him, “Unbelieving generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him to me.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩgatwarĩra Jesũ. Rĩrĩa ngoma ĩyo thũku yoonire Jesũ, ĩgĩgathiorania o rĩmwe. Gagĩtungumana thĩ, gakĩgaragara, gakĩrutaga mũhũyũ na kanua.
English: They brought him to him, and when he saw him, immediately the spirit convulsed him and he fell on the ground, wallowing and foaming at the mouth.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩũria ithe wa kamwana kau atĩrĩ, “Nĩ hĩndĩ ĩigana atĩa gakoretwo kahaana ũguo?” Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kuuma arĩ o kaana.
English: He asked his father, “How long has it been since this has been happening to him?” He said, “From childhood.

Kikuyu: Na maita maingĩ yanagaikia mwaki-inĩ o na maaĩ-inĩ ĩkoorage. No akorwo nĩ harĩ ũndũ ũngĩka, tũiguĩre tha ũtũteithie.”
English: Often it has cast him both into the fire and into the water to destroy him. But if you can do anything, have compassion on us and help us.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Atĩ ‘korwo ndahota’? Maũndũ mothe nĩmahotekaga kũrĩ mũndũ ũrĩa wĩtĩkĩtie.”
English: Jesus said to him, “If you can believe, all things are possible to him who believes.”

Kikuyu: O rĩmwe ithe wa kamwana kau akĩanĩrĩra, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩndetĩkia, ndeithia harĩa njagĩte gwĩtĩkia.”
English: Immediately the father of the child cried out with tears, “I believe. Help my unbelief!”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oonire atĩ kĩrĩndĩ nĩgĩatengʼerete gĩũke hau, agĩkũũma ngoma ĩyo thũku. Akĩmĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee ngoma ĩno thũku ũtaiguaga na ũtaaragia, nĩndagwatha uume thĩinĩ wa kamwana gaka, na ndũkanacooke gũtoonya thĩinĩ wako rĩngĩ.”
English: When Jesus saw that a multitude came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to him, “You mute and deaf spirit, I command you, come out of him, and never enter him again!”

Kikuyu: Ngoma ĩyo thũku ĩgĩkayũrũrũka, ĩgĩgathiorania na hinya, na ĩkiuma. Kamwana kau gakĩhaana ta karĩ gakuũ, o nginya andũ aingĩ makiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ gakuũ.”
English: After crying out and convulsing him greatly, it came out of him. The boy became like one dead, so much that most of them said, “He is dead.”

Kikuyu: No Jesũ agĩkanyiita guoko, agĩgokĩria, nako gakĩrũgama.
English: But Jesus took him by the hand and raised him up; and he arose.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa Jesũ gũtoonya nyũmba, arutwo ake makĩmũũrĩria keheri-inĩ atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmire ithuĩ tũremwo nĩ kũingata ngoma ĩyo?”
English: When he had come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, “Why couldn’t we cast it out?”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ngoma ya mũthemba ta ũcio ndĩngiuma na njĩra ĩngĩ tiga o ya mahooya (o na kwĩima irio).” (Nĩ harĩ maandĩko mangĩ matakoragwo na ũhoro ũyũ wa kwĩima irio).
English: He said to them, “This kind can come out by nothing but by prayer and fasting.”

Kikuyu: Nao makiuma kũu magĩtuĩkanĩria bũrũri wa Galili, na Jesũ ndeendaga mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ amenye kũrĩa marĩ,
English: They went out from there and passed through Galilee. He didn’t want anyone to know it,

Kikuyu: tondũ nĩarutaga arutwo ake. Nake akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩegũkunyanĩrwo aneanwo moko-inĩ ma andũ, nao nĩmakamũũraga, no thuutha wa mĩthenya ĩtatũ nĩakariũka.”
English: for he was teaching his disciples, and said to them, “The Son of Man is being handed over to the hands of men, and they will kill him; and when he is killed, on the third day he will rise again.”

Kikuyu: No matiigana gũtaũkĩrwo nĩ ũrĩa oigaga, na magĩĩtigĩra kũmũũria ũhoro ũcio.
English: But they didn’t understand the saying, and were afraid to ask him.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkinya Kaperinaumu. Na rĩrĩa aarĩ nyũmba thĩinĩ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũgũkararanagĩria kĩĩ tũrĩ njĩra-inĩ?”
English: He came to Capernaum, and when he was in the house he asked them, “What were you arguing among yourselves on the way?”

Kikuyu: No-o magĩkira tondũ marĩ njĩra-inĩ nĩmakararanagia makĩũranagia nũũ mũnene kũrĩ arĩa angĩ.
English: But they were silent, for they had disputed with one another on the way about who was the greatest.

Kikuyu: Na aikara thĩ, Jesũ agĩĩta arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũrĩa wothe ũngĩenda gũtuĩka wa mbere, no nginya akorwo arĩ wa thuutha wa arĩa angĩ na atuĩke ndungata ya andũ othe.”
English: He sat down and called the twelve; and he said to them, “If any man wants to be first, he shall be last of all, and servant of all.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩrehe kaana agĩkarũgamia gatagatĩ kao. Na aarĩkia gũkaiyũkia moko-inĩ make, akĩmeera atĩrĩ,
English: He took a little child and set him in the middle of them. Taking him in his arms, he said to them,

Kikuyu: “Ũrĩa wothe ũngĩamũkĩra kaana kamwe ta gaka thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, nĩ niĩ aamũkagĩra; na ũrĩa ũngĩnyamũkĩra niĩ to niĩ aamũkagĩra, no nĩamũkagĩra ũrĩa wandũmire.”
English: “Whoever receives one such little child in my name receives me; and whoever receives me, doesn’t receive me, but him who sent me.”

Kikuyu: Johana akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, nĩtũronire mũndũ akĩingata ndaimono thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩaku tũramwĩra atige, tondũ ti ũmwe witũ.”
English: John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone who doesn’t follow us casting out demons in your name; and we forbade him, because he doesn’t follow us.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũtikamũgirie! Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩringa kĩama thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa na thuutha ũcio ahote gũcooka kwaria ũũru wakwa,
English: But Jesus said, “Don’t forbid him, for there is no one who will do a mighty work in my name and be able quickly to speak evil of me.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ũrĩa ũtarĩ ũthũ na ithuĩ nĩ ũmwe witũ.
English: For whoever is not against us is on our side.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũkaamũhe gĩkombe kĩa maaĩ thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa nĩ ũndũ mũrĩ a Kristũ, ndarĩ hĩndĩ akoorwo nĩ igai rĩake.”
English: For whoever will give you a cup of water to drink in my name because you are Christ’s, most certainly I tell you, he will in no way lose his reward.

Kikuyu: “Na mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũgaatũma kamwe ga twana tũtũ tũnjĩtĩkĩtie kehie, wega nĩ mũndũ ũcio ohererwo ihiga rĩa gĩthĩi ngingo na aikio thĩinĩ wa iria.
English: “Whoever will cause one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if he were thrown into the sea with a millstone hung around his neck.

Kikuyu: Guoko gwaku kũngĩtũma wĩhie, gũtinie. Nĩ kaba ũtoonye muoyo-inĩ wĩ kĩonje,
English: If your hand causes you to stumble, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life maimed, rather than having your two hands to go into Gehenna, † into the unquenchable fire,

Kikuyu: gũkĩra ũthiĩ Jehanamu kũrĩa kũrĩ mwaki ũtahoraga ũrĩ na moko meerĩ.
English: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’ *‡

Kikuyu: Nakuo kũgũrũ gwaku kũngĩtũma wĩhie, gũtinie. Nĩ kaba ũtoonye muoyo-inĩ ũrĩ gĩthua, gũkĩra ũikio Jehanamu ũrĩ na magũrũ meerĩ.
English: If your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life lame, rather than having your two feet to be cast into Gehenna, § into the fire that will never be quenched—

Kikuyu: Kũu kĩgunyũ kĩrĩa kĩmarĩĩaga gĩtikuaga, naguo mwaki ũrĩa ũmacinaga ndũhoraga.
English: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’ †

Kikuyu: Na riitho rĩaku rĩngĩtũma wĩhie, rĩkũũre. Nĩ kaba ũtoonye ũthamaki- inĩ wa Ngai ũrĩ na riitho rĩmwe gũkĩra ũikio Jehanamu ũrĩ na maitho meerĩ,
English: If your eye causes you to stumble, throw it out. It is better for you to enter into God’s Kingdom with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into the Gehenna‡ of fire,

Kikuyu: kũu “ ‘kĩgunyũ kĩrĩa kĩmarĩĩaga gĩtikuaga, naguo mwaki ũrĩa ũmacinaga ndũhoraga.’
English: ‘where their worm doesn’t die, and the fire is not quenched.’ *

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe agekĩrwo mũcamo wa cumbĩ nĩ mwaki, (na igongona o rĩothe rĩĩkĩrwo mũcamo na cumbĩ).
English: For everyone will be salted with fire, and every sacrifice will be seasoned with salt.

Kikuyu: “Cumbĩ nĩ mwega, no cumbĩ ũngĩkorwo nĩũthirĩte mũcamo waguo-rĩ, ũngĩcooka gũcamithio ta cumbĩ na kĩ? Tuĩkai cumbĩ, na mũikaranagie na thayũ.”
English: Salt is good, but if the salt has lost its saltiness, with what will you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcooka akiuma kũu agĩthiĩ bũrũri wa Judea na mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ wa Jorodani. O rĩngĩ andũ ikundi magĩũka kũrĩ we, nake akĩmaruta ũhoro o ta ũrĩa aamenyerete gwĩka.
English: He arose from there and came into the borders of Judea and beyond the Jordan. Multitudes came together to him again. As he usually did, he was again teaching them.

Kikuyu: Na Afarisai amwe magĩũka kũrĩ we makĩenda kũmũgeria, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Watho nĩwĩtĩkĩrĩtie mũndũ atigane na mũtumia wake?”
English: Pharisees came to him testing him, and asked him, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?”

Kikuyu: Nake agĩcookia ũhoro, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Musa aamwathire mwĩkage atĩa?”
English: He answered, “What did Moses command you?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Musa nĩetĩkagĩria mũndũ aandĩkĩre mũtumia wake marũa ma gũtigana nake, amũte.”
English: They said, “Moses allowed a certificate of divorce to be written, and to divorce her.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Musa aamwandĩkĩire watho ũcio tondũ wa ũrĩa muomĩtie ngoro cianyu.
English: But Jesus said to them, “For your hardness of heart, he wrote you this commandment.

Kikuyu: No kuuma o kĩambĩrĩria, Ngai ‘ombire mũndũ mũrũme na mũndũ-wa-nja’.
English: But from the beginning of the creation, God made them male and female.*

Kikuyu: ‘Gĩkĩ nĩkĩo gĩtũmaga mũndũ mũrũme atige ithe na nyina na anyiitane na mũtumia wake,
English: For this cause a man will leave his father and mother, and will join to his wife,

Kikuyu: nao eerĩ magatuĩka mwĩrĩ ũmwe.’ Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, acio ti andũ eerĩ rĩngĩ, no nĩ mũndũ ũmwe.
English: and the two will become one flesh,* so that they are no longer two, but one flesh.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, arĩa Ngai anyiitithanĩtie, mũndũ ndakanamatigithanie.”
English: What therefore God has joined together, let no man separate.”

Kikuyu: Na maatoonya nyũmba thĩinĩ, arutwo makĩũria Jesũ ũhoro ũcio o rĩngĩ.
English: In the house, his disciples asked him again about the same matter.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ o wothe angĩtigana na mũtumia wake acooke ahikie mũtumia ũngĩ, mũndũ ũcio nĩ atharĩtie.
English: He said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.

Kikuyu: Nake mũtumia angĩtigana na mũthuuriwe, ahikĩre mũndũ mũrũme ũngĩ, o nake nĩ atharĩtie.”
English: If a woman herself divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.”

Kikuyu: Nao andũ makĩrehera Jesũ twana tũnini nĩguo atũhutie, no arutwo ake makĩmakaania.
English: They were bringing to him little children, that he should touch them, but the disciples rebuked those who were bringing them.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oonire ũguo, akĩrakara, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rekei twana tũu tũũke kũrĩ niĩ, tigai gũtũgiria, nĩgũkorwo ũthamaki wa Ngai nĩ wa arĩa matariĩ ta tuo.
English: But when Jesus saw it, he was moved with indignation and said to them, “Allow the little children to come to me! Don’t forbid them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũrĩa wothe ũtakamũkĩra ũthamaki wa Ngai ta kaana ndakaũtoonya o na atĩa.”
English: Most certainly I tell you, whoever will not receive God’s Kingdom like a little child, he will in no way enter into it.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩoya twana tũu, agĩtũigĩrĩra moko, agĩtũrathima.
English: He took them in his arms and blessed them, laying his hands on them.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Jesũ oimagaraga, mũndũ ũmwe nĩatengʼerire harĩ we, agĩturia ndu mbere yake, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani mwega, ingĩĩka atĩa nĩguo ngaagaya muoyo wa tene na tene?”
English: As he was going out into the way, one ran to him, knelt before him, and asked him, “Good Teacher, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũkũnjĩta mwega nĩkĩ? Gũtirĩ o na ũmwe mwega, tiga o Ngai we wiki.
English: Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good except one—God.

Kikuyu: Wee nĩ ũũĩ ũrĩa maathani moigĩte: ‘Ndũkanoragane, na ndũkanatharanie, na ndũkanaiye, na ndũkanaigĩrĩre mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ kĩgeenyo, na ndũkanaheenanie, na tĩĩa thoguo na nyũkwa.’ ”
English: You know the commandments: ‘Do not murder,’ ‘Do not commit adultery,’ ‘Do not steal,’ ‘Do not give false testimony,’ ‘Do not defraud,’ ‘Honor your father and mother.’ ”*

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, macio mothe ndũire ndĩmarũmĩtie kuuma ndĩ mũnini.”
English: He said to him, “Teacher, I have observed all these things from my youth.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ amũrora, akĩmwenda, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kũrĩ ũndũ ũmwe ũtigairie: Thiĩ wendie indo ciaku ciothe iria ũrĩ nacio ũhe athĩĩni mbeeca icio, na nĩũkagĩa na mũthithũ igũrũ. Ũcooke ũũke, ũnũmĩrĩre.”
English: Jesus looking at him loved him, and said to him, “One thing you lack. Go, sell whatever you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me, taking up the cross.”

Kikuyu: Mwanake ũcio agĩtukia gĩthiithi. Agĩthiĩ arĩ na kĩeha, tondũ aarĩ na ũtonga mũingĩ.
English: But his face fell at that saying, and he went away sorrowful, for he was one who had great possessions.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩĩhũgũra, akĩĩra arutwo ake atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ nĩ ũndũ wĩ hinya gĩtonga gũtoonya ũthamaki- inĩ wa Ngai-ĩ!”
English: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “How difficult it is for those who have riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!”

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo makĩgega maigua ciugo ciake. No Jesũ akiuga o rĩngĩ atĩrĩ, “Ciana, kaĩ nĩ ũndũ wĩ hinya gũtoonya ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai-ĩ!
English: The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus answered again, “Children, how hard it is for those who trust in riches to enter into God’s Kingdom!

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũhũthũ ngamĩĩra kũhungura irima rĩa cindano gũkĩra gĩtonga gũtoonya ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai.”
English: It is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”

Kikuyu: Arutwo ake magĩkĩrĩrĩria kũgega, makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Nũũ ũngĩkĩhonoka?”
English: They were exceedingly astonished, saying to him, “Then who can be saved?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmarora, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Harĩ mũndũ ũndũ ũyũ ndũngĩhoteka, no harĩ Ngai tiguo; maũndũ mothe nĩ mahotekaga nĩ Ngai.”
English: Jesus, looking at them, said, “With men it is impossible, but not with God, for all things are possible with God.”

Kikuyu: Petero akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtigĩte maũndũ mothe tũgakũrũmĩrĩra!”
English: Peter began to tell him, “Behold, we have left all and have followed you.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũtigĩte mũciĩ kana ariũ a nyina, kana aarĩ a nyina, kana nyina, kana ithe, kana ciana, o na kana ithaka nĩ ũndũ wakwa, na nĩ ũndũ wa Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega,
English: Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, there is no one who has left house, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or land, for my sake, and for the sake of the Good News,

Kikuyu: ũtakaheo ũtonga maita igana matukũ maya tũrĩ, na aheo maita igana ma nyũmba, na ariũ a nyina, na aarĩ a nyina, na manyina, na ciana o na ithaka, na hamwe na macio, mĩnyamaro. Na ihinda rĩrĩa rĩgooka aheo muoyo wa tene na tene.
English: but he will receive one hundred times more now in this time: houses, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and land, with persecutions; and in the age to come eternal life.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, andũ aingĩ arĩa marĩ mbere nĩmagatuĩka a thuutha, na a thuutha matuĩke a mbere.”
English: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa maarĩ njĩra-inĩ makĩambata merekeire Jerusalemu, Jesũ amatongoretie, arutwo ake nĩmagegire, nao arĩa maamarũmĩrĩire magĩĩtigĩra. O rĩngĩ Jesũ agĩtwara arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ keheri-inĩ, akĩambĩrĩria kũmeera maũndũ marĩa maakirie kũmũkora.
English: They were on the way, going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus was going in front of them, and they were amazed; and those who followed were afraid. He again took the twelve, and began to tell them the things that were going to happen to him.

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũkwambata tũthiĩ Jerusalemu, nake Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩegũkunyanĩrwo kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na kũrĩ arutani a watho. Nao mamũtuĩre kũũragwo na mamũneane kũrĩ andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ,
English: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem. The Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and the scribes. They will condemn him to death, and will deliver him to the Gentiles.

Kikuyu: nao mamũnyũrũrie na mamũtuĩre mata, na mamũhũũre iboko o na mamũũrage. No thuutha wa mĩthenya ĩtatũ nĩakariũka.”
English: They will mock him, spit on him, scourge him, and kill him. On the third day he will rise again.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jakubu na Johana, ariũ a Zebedi, magĩũka kũrĩ Jesũ. Makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, tũkwenda ũtwĩkĩre o ũrĩa tũgũkũhooya.”
English: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came near to him, saying, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we will ask.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũkwenda ndĩmwĩkĩre atĩa?”
English: He said to them, “What do you want me to do for you?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũtwĩtĩkĩrie tũgaikara ũmwe guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũrĩo na ũrĩa ũngĩ guoko-inĩ gwaku kwa ũmotho riiri-inĩ waku.”
English: They said to him, “Grant to us that we may sit, one at your right hand and one at your left hand, in your glory.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũtiũĩ kĩrĩa mũrahooya. Mwahota kũnyuĩra gĩkombe kĩrĩa niĩ ngũnyuĩra, kana mũbatithio na ũbatithio ũrĩa ngũbatithio naguo?”
English: But Jesus said to them, “You don’t know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?”

Kikuyu: Nao magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ no tũhote.” Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Gĩkombe kĩrĩa ngũnyuĩra nĩ mũkaanyuĩra, o na mũbatithio na ũbatithio ũrĩa ngũbatithio naguo.
English: They said to him, “We are able.” Jesus said to them, “You shall indeed drink the cup that I drink, and you shall be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with;

Kikuyu: No ha ũhoro wa gũikara guoko-inĩ gwakwa kwa ũrĩo kana kwa ũmotho ti niĩ heanaga. Itĩ icio ikaaheo arĩa mathondekeirwo cio.”
English: but to sit at my right hand and at my left hand is not mine to give, but for whom it has been prepared.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa arutwo acio angĩ ikũmi maiguire ũhoro ũcio, makĩrakarĩra Jakubu na Johana.
English: When the ten heard it, they began to be indignant toward James and John.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeeta hamwe akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩ mũũĩ atĩ andũ arĩa matuuagwo aathani a andũ-a-Ndũrĩrĩ nĩmetũũgagĩria igũrũ rĩao, nao anene ao nĩmamaathaga.
English: Jesus summoned them and said to them, “You know that they who are recognized as rulers over the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, inyuĩ mũtitariĩ ũguo. Handũ ha ũguo, ũrĩa wothe ũngĩenda gũtuĩka mũnene thĩinĩ wanyu no nginya atuĩke ndungata yanyu,
English: But it shall not be so among you, but whoever wants to become great among you shall be your servant.

Kikuyu: nake ũrĩa wothe ũkwenda gũtuĩka wa mbere thĩinĩ wanyu, no nginya atuĩke ngombo ya andũ othe.
English: Whoever of you wants to become first among you shall be bondservant of all.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo o na Mũrũ wa Mũndũ ndookire gũtungatĩrwo, no ookire gũtungata na arute muoyo wake nĩguo arĩhe thogora wa gũkũũra andũ aingĩ.”
English: For the Son of Man also came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkinya Jeriko. Na rĩrĩa Jesũ na arutwo ake, hamwe na kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene moimaga itũũra- inĩ rĩu, mũndũ warĩ mũtumumu wetagwo Baritimayo (ũguo nĩ kuuga mũrũ wa Timayo), aikarĩte thĩ mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra akĩhooya thendi.
English: They came to Jericho. As he went out from Jericho with his disciples and a great multitude, the son of Timaeus, Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, was sitting by the road.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aiguire atĩ nĩ Jesũ wa Nazarethi, akĩambĩrĩria kwanĩrĩra akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Jesũ, Mũrũ wa Daudi, njiguĩra tha!”
English: When he heard that it was Jesus the Nazarene, he began to cry out and say, “Jesus, you son of David, have mercy on me!”

Kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ makĩmũkaania, makĩmwĩra akire, nowe agĩkĩrĩrĩria kwanĩrĩra, akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa Daudi, njiguĩra tha!”
English: Many rebuked him, that he should be quiet, but he cried out much more, “You son of David, have mercy on me!”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩrũgama, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwĩtei.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩĩta mũndũ ũcio warĩ mũtumumu, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wĩyũmĩrĩrie! Rũgama! Nĩaragwĩta.”
English: Jesus stood still and said, “Call him.” They called the blind man, saying to him, “Cheer up! Get up. He is calling you!”

Kikuyu: Agĩteania nguo yake ya igũrũ, agĩũkĩra na ihenya agĩthiĩ harĩ Jesũ.
English: He, casting away his cloak, sprang up, and came to Jesus.

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũkwenda ngwĩkĩre atĩa?” Nake mũndũ ũcio warĩ mũtumumu akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, ngwenda o njooke kuona.”
English: Jesus asked him, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man said to him, “Rabboni,† that I may see again.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wĩthiĩre, wĩtĩkio waku nĩguo watũma ũhone.” O rĩmwe akĩambĩrĩria kuona, nake akĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ.
English: Jesus said to him, “Go your way. Your faith has made you well.” Immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus on the way.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, maakuhĩrĩria itũũra rĩa Jerusalemu, maakinya matũũra ma Bethifage na Bethania hau Kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ-rĩ, Jesũ agĩtũma arutwo ake eerĩ,
English: When they came near to Jerusalem, to Bethsphage† and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples

Kikuyu: akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi itũũra rĩĩrĩa rĩrĩ mbere yanyu, na mwatoonya o ũguo, nĩmũkuona njaũ ya ndigiri yohetwo hau, nayo ndĩrĩ yakuua mũndũ. Mĩohorei mũmĩrehe.
English: and said to them, “Go your way into the village that is opposite you. Immediately as you enter into it, you will find a young donkey tied, on which no one has sat. Untie him and bring him.

Kikuyu: Na mũngĩũrio nĩ mũndũ atĩrĩ, ‘Mũreka ũguo nĩkĩ?’ Mwĩrei atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathani nĩabatarĩtio nĩyo na nĩekũmĩcookia haha o narua.’ ”
English: If anyone asks you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ say, ‘The Lord needs him;’ and immediately he will send him back here.”

Kikuyu: Nao magĩthiĩ magĩkora njaũ ya ndigiri yohetwo mũromo-inĩ handũ nja, hakuhĩ na njĩra. Na rĩrĩa maamĩohoraga-rĩ,
English: They went away, and found a young donkey tied at the door outside in the open street, and they untied him.

Kikuyu: andũ arĩa maarũngiĩ ho makĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũrohora njaũ ĩyo nĩkĩ?”
English: Some of those who stood there asked them, “What are you doing, untying the young donkey?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmacookeria o ũrĩa Jesũ aamerĩte, nao andũ acio makĩmetĩkĩria mathiĩ nayo.
English: They said to them just as Jesus had said, and they let them go.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maatwarĩire Jesũ njaũ ĩyo, makĩmĩtandĩka nguo ciao nake Jesũ agĩkuuo nĩyo.
English: They brought the young donkey to Jesus and threw their garments on it, and Jesus sat on it.

Kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ makĩara nguo ciao njĩra- inĩ, nao arĩa angĩ makĩara mathĩgĩ marĩa maatuĩte mĩgũnda-inĩ.
English: Many spread their garments on the way, and others were cutting down branches from the trees and spreading them on the road.

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maatongoretie na arĩa maamumĩte thuutha makĩanĩrĩra makiuga atĩrĩ, “Hosana!”* “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ ũrĩa ũgũũka na rĩĩtwa rĩa Mwathani!”
English: Those who went in front and those who followed cried out, “Hosanna!‡ Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!*

Kikuyu: “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ ũthamaki ũrĩa ũgũũka, ũrĩa wa ithe witũ Daudi!” “Hosana igũrũ o igũrũ!”
English: Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that is coming in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ aatoonya Jerusalemu, agĩthiĩ hekarũ-inĩ. Na aarĩkia kwĩrorangĩra maũndũ mothe, akiumagara agĩthiĩ Bethania hamwe na arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ, tondũ kwarĩ hwaĩ-inĩ.
English: Jesus entered into the temple in Jerusalem. When he had looked around at everything, it being now evening, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ rĩrĩa moimaga Bethania, Jesũ akĩigua ahũtiĩ.
English: The next day, when they had come out from Bethany, he was hungry.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩona mũkũyũ warĩ haraaya ũrĩ na mathangũ maruru agĩthiĩ kũrora kana warĩ na ngũyũ. Aakinya harĩ guo, agĩkora ndwarĩ na ngũyũ tiga o mathangũ, tondũ ndĩarĩ hĩndĩ ya mĩkũyũ gũciara.
English: Seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came to see if perhaps he might find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩĩra mũkũyũ ũcio atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ndakanarĩe maciaro maku nginya tene.” Nao arutwo ake makĩigua akiuga ũguo.
English: Jesus told it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again!” and his disciples heard it.

Kikuyu: Na maakinya Jerusalemu, Jesũ agĩtoonya hekarũ thĩinĩ,† akĩambĩrĩria kũrutũrũra arĩa maagũraga, na arĩa meendanagĩria kuo. Akĩngʼaũrania metha cia arĩa maakũũranagia mbeeca, o na itĩ cia arĩa meendagia ndutura,
English: They came to Jerusalem, and Jesus entered into the temple and began to throw out those who sold and those who bought in the temple, and overthrew the money changers’ tables and the seats of those who sold the doves.

Kikuyu: na ndangĩetĩkĩririe mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ahĩtũkĩrie kĩndũ gĩa kwendia kũu nja cia hekarũ.‡
English: He would not allow anyone to carry a container through the temple.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aamarutaga, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Githĩ ũhoro ndwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, “ ‘Nyũmba yakwa ĩgeetagwo nyũmba ya mahooya ya kũhooyagĩrwo nĩ ndũrĩrĩ ciothe’§? No inyuĩ mũmĩtuĩte ‘ngurunga ya atunyani’.”
English: He taught, saying to them, “Isn’t it written, ‘My house will be called a house of prayer for all the nations’?* But you have made it a den of robbers!”*

Kikuyu: Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na arutani a watho maigua ũguo, makĩambĩrĩria gũcaria ũrĩa mangĩmũũraga, nĩ ũndũ nĩmamwĩtigagĩra, tondũ kĩrĩndĩ gĩothe nĩkĩagegetio nĩ ũrutani wake.
English: The chief priests and the scribes heard it, and sought how they might destroy him. For they feared him, because all the multitude was astonished at his teaching.

Kikuyu: Gwakinya hwaĩ-inĩ, Jesũ na arutwo ake makiuma itũũra rĩu.
English: When evening came, he went out of the city.

Kikuyu: Rũciinĩ, makĩhĩtũka makĩona mũkũyũ ũrĩa Jesũ aarumĩĩte ũũmĩte wothe nginya mĩri.
English: As they passed by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered away from the roots.

Kikuyu: Petero akĩririkana ũhoro wa mũkũyũ ũcio, akĩĩra Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Rabii, ta rora! Mũkũyũ ũrĩa ũrarumire-rĩ, nĩũũmĩte!”
English: Peter, remembering, said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree which you cursed has withered away.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ĩtĩkiai Ngai.
English: Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, mũndũ angĩkeera kĩrĩma gĩkĩ atĩrĩ, ‘Thiĩ, wĩikie iria-inĩ,’ na ndagathanganie ngoro-inĩ yake, no etĩkie atĩ ũrĩa oiga nĩũgwĩkĩka-rĩ, nĩagekĩrwo ũndũ ũcio.
English: For most certainly I tell you, whoever may tell this mountain, ‘Be taken up and cast into the sea,’ and doesn’t doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says is happening, he shall have whatever he says.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũrĩa wothe mũrĩhooyaga mũheo, ĩtĩkagiai atĩ nĩ mũrĩkĩtie kũwamũkĩra, naguo nĩũrĩtuĩkaga wanyu.
English: Therefore I tell you, all things whatever you pray and ask for, believe that you have received them, and you shall have them.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa mwarũgama mũhooe, mũrekanagĩre angĩkorwo mũrĩ ũthũ na mũndũ, nĩgeetha Ithe wanyu ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ o nake amũrekagĩre mehia manyu.” (
English: Whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone; so that your Father, who is in heaven, may also forgive you your transgressions.

Kikuyu: No mũngĩkorwo mũtirekanagĩra mehia, nake Ithe wanyu ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ ndarĩmũrekagĩra mehia manyu.)
English: But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your transgressions.”§

Kikuyu: Magĩkinya o rĩngĩ Jerusalemu, na rĩrĩa Jesũ aaceeraga kũu nja cia hekarũ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho, o na athuuri magĩũka kũrĩ we.
English: They came again to Jerusalem, and as he was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to him,

Kikuyu: Makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wĩkaga maũndũ maya na ũhoti ũrĩkũ? Na nũũ wakũheire ũhoti wa gwĩka maũndũ maya?”
English: and they began saying to him, “By what authority do you do these things? Or who gave you this authority to do these things?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩngũmũũria kĩũria kĩmwe. Mwanjookeria, na niĩ nĩngũmwĩra njĩkaga maũndũ maya na ũhoti ũrĩkũ.
English: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question. Answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.

Kikuyu: Ũbatithio wa Johana-rĩ, woimire igũrũ kana kũrĩ andũ? Njookeriai!”
English: The baptism of John—was it from heaven, or from men? Answer me.”

Kikuyu: Makĩaranĩria o ene ũhoro ũcio, makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Tũngiuga atĩ, ‘Woimire igũrũ’, egũtũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Mwakĩregire kũmwĩtĩkia nĩkĩ?’
English: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we should say, ‘From heaven;’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’

Kikuyu: No tũngiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Woimire kũrĩ andũ’ … .” (Nĩmetigagĩra andũ, tondũ andũ othe mooĩ kũna atĩ Johana aarĩ mũnabii.)
English: If we should say, ‘From men’ ”—they feared the people, for all held John to really be a prophet.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩcookeria Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ tũtiũĩ.” Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “O na niĩ ndikũmwĩra njĩkaga maũndũ maya na ũhoti ũrĩkũ.”
English: They answered Jesus, “We don’t know.” Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩambĩrĩria kũmahe ũhoro na ngerekano, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũmwe nĩahaandire mũgũnda wake mĩthabibũ. Agĩaka rũgiri rũgĩthiũrũrũkĩria mũgũnda ũcio, na akĩenja irima rĩa kũhihĩra thabibũ, na agĩaka nyũmba ndaaya na igũrũ ya arangĩri. Agĩcooka agĩkomborithia mũgũnda ũcio kũrĩ arĩmi angĩ, na agĩthiĩ rũgendo.
English: He began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard, put a hedge around it, dug a pit for the wine press, built a tower, rented it out to a farmer, and went into another country.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ya magetha yakinya-rĩ, agĩtũma ndungata yake kũrĩ akombori acio ĩgĩĩre maciaro mamwe ma mũgũnda ũcio wa mĩthabibũ.
English: When it was time, he sent a servant to the farmer to get from the farmer his share of the fruit of the vineyard.

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmĩnyiita makĩmĩhũũra, na makĩmĩingata ĩtarĩ na kĩndũ.
English: They took him, beat him, and sent him away empty.

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmatũmĩra ndungata ĩngĩ; nao magĩtiihia ndungata ĩyo mũtwe na makĩmĩĩka maũndũ ma thoni.
English: Again, he sent another servant to them; and they threw stones at him, wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully treated.

Kikuyu: Na o rĩngĩ agĩtũma ndungata ĩngĩ, nayo makĩmĩũraga. Nĩatũmire ndungata ingĩ nyingĩ, imwe ciacio magĩcihũũra, na iria ingĩ magĩciũraga.
English: Again he sent another, and they killed him, and many others, beating some, and killing some.

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũcio ndaarĩ na mũndũ ũngĩ watigaire wa gũtũma, tiga o mũriũ wake ũrĩa eendete mũno. Akĩmũtũma thuutha wa acio angĩ othe, akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩmegũtĩĩa mũrũ wakwa.’
English: Therefore still having one, his beloved son, he sent him last to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’

Kikuyu: “No akombori acio makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, ‘Ũyũ nĩwe ũkaagaya mũgũnda ũyũ. Ũkai, rekei tũmũũrage, nĩguo igai rĩake rĩgaatuĩka riitũ.’
English: But those farmers said among themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩmũnyiita, makĩmũũraga, makĩmũikia nja ya mũgũnda ũcio wa mĩthabibũ.
English: They took him, killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.

Kikuyu: “Rĩu-rĩ, mwene mũgũnda ũcio ageeka atĩa? Agooka oorage akombori acio, acooke ahe andũ angĩ mũgũnda ũcio.
English: What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ mũtarĩ mwathoma maandĩko maya: “ ‘Ihiga rĩrĩa aaki maaregire, nĩrĩo rĩtuĩkĩte ihiga inene rĩa koine;
English: Haven’t you even read this Scripture: ‘The stone which the builders rejected was made the head of the corner.

Kikuyu: Mwathani nĩwe wĩkĩte ũndũ ũcio, naguo nĩ wa magegania tũkĩwona’?”
English: This was from the Lord. It is marvelous in our eyes’?”*

Kikuyu: Magĩcaria ũrĩa mangĩmũnyiita tondũ nĩmamenyire aaragia ũhoro wao na ngerekano ĩyo, no nĩmetigagĩra kĩrĩndĩ; nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩtigana nake, magĩthiĩ.
English: They tried to seize him, but they feared the multitude; for they perceived that he spoke the parable against them. They left him and went away.

Kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio magĩtũma Afarisai amwe na Aherodia kũrĩ Jesũ nĩguo mamũtege na mĩario yake.
English: They sent some of the Pharisees and the Herodians to him, that they might trap him with words.

Kikuyu: Magĩũka kũrĩ we makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, nĩtũũĩ atĩ ũrĩ mũndũ wa ma. Ndwĩtigagĩra andũ, tondũ ndũrũmbũyagia ũrĩa mahaana; no ũrutanaga ũhoro wa Ngai na ma. Rĩu-rĩ, nĩ kwagĩrĩire kũrutĩra Kaisari mbeeca cia igooti, kana aca?*
English: When they had come, they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest, and don’t defer to anyone; for you aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?

Kikuyu: Tũrutage kana tũtikarutage?” No tondũ Jesũ nĩamenyaga ũhinga wao, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũrenda kũngeria nĩkĩ? Ta ndeherai dinari ndĩmĩone.”
English: Shall we give, or shall we not give?” But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them, “Why do you test me? Bring me a denarius, that I may see it.”

Kikuyu: Makĩmũrehera dinari ĩyo, nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mbica ĩno na rĩĩtwa rĩĩrĩ nĩ cia ũ?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ cia Kaisari.”
English: They brought it. He said to them, “Whose is this image and inscription?” They said to him, “Caesar’s.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Rutagĩrai Kaisari kĩrĩa kĩrĩ gĩake, na mũrutagĩre Ngai kĩrĩa kĩrĩ gĩake.” Nao makĩgegio nĩwe.
English: Jesus answered them, “Give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.” They marveled greatly at him.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Asadukai, arĩa moigaga atĩ gũtirĩ ũhoro wa kũriũka magĩũka kũrĩ Jesũ na makĩmũũria atĩrĩ,
English: Some Sadducees, who say that there is no resurrection, came to him. They asked him, saying,

Kikuyu: “Mũrutani, Musa aatwandĩkĩire atĩ mũrũ wa nyina na mũndũ angĩkua atige mũtumia atarĩ na ciana- rĩ, mũndũ ũcio no nginya ahikie mũtumia ũcio watigwo, aciarĩre mũrũ wa nyina ciana.
English: “Teacher, Moses wrote to us, ‘If a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife behind him, and leaves no children, that his brother should take his wife and raise up offspring for his brother.’

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kwarĩ aanake mũgwanja a nyina ũmwe. Wa mbere akĩhikania na agĩkua, na ndatige ciana.
English: There were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and dying left no offspring.

Kikuyu: Nake wa keerĩ akĩhikia mũtumia ũcio wa ndigwa, no o nake agĩkua na ndatige ciana. O na wa gatatũ nake agĩĩka o ũguo.
English: The second took her, and died, leaving no children behind him. The third likewise;

Kikuyu: Ũhoro wa ma nĩ atĩ, gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wa acio mũgwanja watigire ciana. Thuutha wao othe, mũtumia ũcio o nake agĩkua.
English: and the seven took her and left no children. Last of all the woman also died.

Kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, hĩndĩ ya kũriũka mũtumia ũcio agaakorwo arĩ wa ũ, nĩgũkorwo othe mũgwanja nĩmamũhikĩtie?”
English: In the resurrection, when they rise, whose wife will she be of them? For the seven had her as a wife.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩmũhĩtĩtie tondũ mũtiũĩ Maandĩko kana hinya wa Ngai?
English: Jesus answered them, “Isn’t this because you are mistaken, not knowing the Scriptures nor the power of God?

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa akuũ makaariũka, matikahikania kana mahike; magaatuĩka ta araika arĩa marĩ kũu igũrũ.
English: For when they will rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.

Kikuyu: Naguo ha ũhoro wa akuũ kũriũka-rĩ, kaĩ mũtathomete ibuku-inĩ rĩa Musa, ũhoro ũkoniĩ kĩhinga kĩrĩa kĩahĩaga, na ũrĩa Ngai aamwĩrire atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ nĩ niĩ Ngai wa Iburahĩmu na Ngai wa Isaaka, o na Ngai wa Jakubu?’
English: But about the dead, that they are raised, haven’t you read in the book of Moses about the Bush, how God spoke to him, saying, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?*

Kikuyu: We ti Ngai wa arĩa akuũ, no nĩ wa arĩa marĩ muoyo. Inyuĩ mũhĩtĩtie mũno.”
English: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are therefore badly mistaken.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũrutani ũmwe wa watho nĩokire akĩigua makĩaria. Aamenya atĩ Jesũ nĩamacookeirie icookio rĩega-rĩ, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Harĩ maathani marĩa mangĩ mothe-rĩ, nĩ rĩrĩkũ rĩa bata mũno?”
English: One of the scribes came and heard them questioning together, and knowing that he had answered them well, asked him, “Which commandment is the greatest of all?”

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Rĩathani rĩrĩa rĩa bata mũno nĩ rĩĩrĩ: ‘Thikĩrĩria, inyuĩ Isiraeli, Mwathani Ngai witũ, Mwathani nĩ ũmwe.
English: Jesus answered, “The greatest is: ‘Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.

Kikuyu: Endaga Mwathani Ngai waku na ngoro yaku yothe, na muoyo waku wothe, na meciiria maku mothe, na hinya waku wothe.’
English: You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your mind, and with all your strength.’* This is the first commandment.

Kikuyu: Narĩo rĩa keerĩ nĩ rĩrĩ: ‘Endaga mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ o ta ũrĩa wĩendete wee mwene.’ Gũtirĩ rĩathani rĩngĩ inene kũrĩ macio.”
English: The second is like this: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’* There is no other commandment greater than these.”

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ ũcio agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, ti-itherũ nĩwoiga wega atĩ Ngai nĩ ũmwe na gũtirĩ ũngĩ tiga we!
English: The scribe said to him, “Truly, teacher, you have said well that he is one, and there is none other but he;

Kikuyu: Ningĩ ũmwende na ngoro yaku yothe, o na ũmenyo waku wothe, o na hinya waku wothe; o nakuo kwenda mũndũ ũrĩa ũngĩ o ta ũrĩa wĩendete wee mwene, ũcio nĩ ũndũ ũrĩ bata gũkĩra maruta mothe ma njino na magongona.”
English: and to love him with all the heart, with all the understanding, all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbor as himself, is more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oonire nĩacookia wega, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee-rĩ, ndũrĩ haraaya na ũthamaki wa Ngai.” Na kuuma hĩndĩ ĩyo gũtirĩ mũndũ wacookire kũũmĩrĩria kũmũũria ciũria ingĩ.
English: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from God’s Kingdom.” No one dared ask him any question after that.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jesũ akĩrutana nja-inĩ cia hekarũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga arutani a watho moige atĩ Kristũ nĩ mũrũ wa Daudi?
English: Jesus responded, as he taught in the temple, “How is it that the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?

Kikuyu: Daudi we mwene, akĩaria arĩ na Roho Mũtheru, oigire atĩrĩ: “ ‘Mwathani eerire Mwathani wakwa atĩrĩ: “ikara guoko-inĩ gwakwa kwa ũrĩo nginya ngaiga thũ ciaku rungu rwa makinya maku.” ’
English: For David himself said in the Holy Spirit, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.” ’*

Kikuyu: Daudi we mwene aramwĩta ‘Mwathani.’ Angĩgĩtuĩka mũrũwe atĩa?” Na nĩ haarĩ na gĩkundi kĩnene kĩa andũ kĩamũthikagĩrĩria gĩkenete.
English: Therefore David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be his son?” The common people heard him gladly.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jesũ akĩrutana, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwĩmenyagĩrĩrei arutani a watho. Mendete gũthiĩ mehumbĩte nguo iria ndaaya† na kũgeithagio nĩ andũ othe rĩrĩa mekũgera ndũnyũ-inĩ,
English: In his teaching he said to them, “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk in long robes, and to get greetings in the marketplaces,

Kikuyu: na makenda gũikaragĩra itĩ iria cia bata thunagogi-inĩ, na gũikara handũ ha andũ arĩa atĩĩku maruga-inĩ.
English: and to get the best seats in the synagogues and the best places at feasts,

Kikuyu: Na no-o matoonyagĩrĩra nyũmba cia atumia a ndigwa makamatunya indo ciao, na nĩguo meyonanie, makahooya mahooya maraihu. Andũ ta acio nĩ makaaherithio mũno makĩria.”
English: those who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩikara thĩ angʼetheire harĩa heekagĩrwo maruta,‡ akĩĩrorera ũrĩa gĩkundi kĩu kĩahothaga. Andũ aingĩ itonga makĩhotha mbeeca nyingĩ.
English: Jesus sat down opposite the treasury and saw how the multitude cast money into the treasury. Many who were rich cast in much.

Kikuyu: No mũtumia ũmwe wa ndigwa warĩ mũthĩĩni agĩũka akĩhotha tũthendi twĩrĩ tũnini mũno twa gĩcango tũrĩa twarĩ mũigana wa mbeeca ĩmwe.§
English: A poor widow came and she cast in two small brass coins,† which equal a quadrans coin.‡

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩĩta arutwo ake harĩ we, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, mũtumia ũyũ wa ndigwa mũthĩĩni nĩekĩra mbeeca nyingĩ kĩgĩĩna-inĩ gũkĩra andũ arĩa angĩ othe.
English: He called his disciples to himself and said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, this poor widow gave more than all those who are giving into the treasury,

Kikuyu: Acio angĩ othe maheana kũringananaũingĩwaũtongawao, nomũtumiaũyũaheanakĩrĩagĩotheoimanakĩo, onaarĩmũthĩĩni- rĩ, aheana mũthithũ wake wothe.”
English: for they all gave out of their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, gave all that she had to live on.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Jesũ oimaga hekarũ-inĩ, ũmwe wa arutwo ake akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, ta rora! Mahiga maya kaĩ nĩ manene-ĩ!* Kaĩ nyũmba ici nĩ thaka-ĩ!”
English: As he went out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, see what kind of stones and what kind of buildings!”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩũrona nyũmba ici nene ũũ? Gũtirĩ ihiga o na rĩmwe rĩa maya rĩgaakorwo rĩrĩ igũrũ wa rĩrĩa rĩngĩ; mothe nĩmakamomorwo.”
English: Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There will not be left here one stone on another, which will not be thrown down.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ aikarĩte thĩ kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ angʼetheire hekarũ-rĩ, Petero na Jakubu na Johana na Anderea makĩmũũria marĩ keheri-inĩ atĩrĩ,
English: As he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,

Kikuyu: “Ta twĩre, maũndũ macio mageekĩka rĩ? Na kĩmenyithia atĩ maũndũ macio marĩ hakuhĩ gwĩkĩka nĩ kĩrĩkũ?”
English: “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are all about to be fulfilled?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mwĩmenyagĩrĩrei mũtikanaheenio nĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ.
English: Jesus, answering, began to tell them, “Be careful that no one leads you astray.

Kikuyu: Andũ aingĩ nĩ magooka na rĩĩtwa rĩakwa makiugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Niĩ nĩ niĩ Kristũ,’ na nĩmakaheenia andũ aingĩ.
English: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’† and will lead many astray.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mũkaigua mbaara na mũhuhu wa mbaara mũtikanamake. Maũndũ ta macio no nginya mekĩke, no rĩrĩ, ithirĩro ti rĩkinyu.
English: “When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, don’t be troubled. For those must happen, but the end is not yet.

Kikuyu: Rũrĩrĩ nĩrũgookĩrĩra rũrĩa rũngĩ, na ũthamaki ũũkĩrĩre ũthamaki ũrĩa ũngĩ. Nĩgũkaagĩa na ithingithia kũndũ na kũndũ, o na kũgĩe ngʼaragu. Maũndũ maya nĩ kĩambĩrĩria kĩa ruo ta rwa mũndũ-wa-nja akĩrũmwo.
English: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places. There will be famines and troubles. These things are the beginning of birth pains.

Kikuyu: “No inyuĩ-rĩ, ikaragai mwĩiguĩte. Nĩmũkaneanwo kũrĩ ciama cia athuuri na mũhũũrwo iboko thĩinĩ wa thunagogi. Nĩmũkarũgama mbere ya aathani na athamaki nĩ ũndũ wakwa, mũtuĩke aira akwakũrĩo.
English: “But watch yourselves, for they will deliver you up to councils. You will be beaten in synagogues. You will stand before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony to them.

Kikuyu: NaguoŨhoro-ũrĩa-Mweganonginyawambeũhunjiokũrĩndũrĩrĩciothe.
English: The Good News must first be preached to all the nations.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩarĩothe mũkaanyiitwo mũtwarwo mũgaciirithio, mũtikanetange mũgĩĩciiria ũrĩa mũkoiga. Inyuĩ mũkoiga o ũrĩa mũkaahotithio hĩndĩ ĩyo, nĩgũkorwo ti inyuĩ mũgaakorwo mũkĩaria, no nĩ Roho mũtheru.
English: When they lead you away and deliver you up, don’t be anxious beforehand or premeditate what you will say, but say whatever will be given you in that hour. For it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ nĩagakunyanĩra mũrũ wa nyina ooragwo, na ithe wa mũndũ akunyanĩre mwana wake, o na ciana nĩikaremera aciari a cio na imoragithie.
English: “Brother will deliver up brother to death, and the father his child. Children will rise up against parents and cause them to be put to death.

Kikuyu: Andũ othe nĩmakamũmena nĩ ũndũ wakwa, no ũrĩa ũgaakirĩrĩria nginya hĩndĩ ya mũthia nĩakahonoka.
English: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake, but he who endures to the end will be saved.

Kikuyu: “Rĩrĩa mũkoona kĩrĩa kĩrĩ thaahu na gĩa kũrehe ihooru kĩarũgama harĩa gĩtagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũrũgama-rĩ, (ũrĩa ũrathoma nĩamenye ũhoro ũcio), hĩndĩ ĩyo arĩa marĩ Judea nĩmoorĩre irĩma-inĩ.
English: “But when you see the abomination of desolation,* spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not” (let the reader understand), “then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains,

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũrĩa wothe ũgaakorwo arĩ nyũmba yake igũrũ ndakanaharũrũke kana atoonye kũruta kĩndũ nyũmba.
English: and let him who is on the housetop not go down, nor enter in, to take anything out of his house.

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ ũrĩa ũgaakorwo arĩ mũgũnda ndagacooke mũciĩ kũgĩĩra nguo ciake.
English: Let him who is in the field not return back to take his cloak.

Kikuyu: Kaĩ atumia arĩa magaakorwo marĩ na nda na arĩa magaakorwo makĩongithia hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmagakorwo marĩ na haaro-ĩ!
English: But woe to those who are with child and to those who nurse babies in those days!

Kikuyu: Hooyagai Ngai atĩ maũndũ macio matikanakinye hĩndĩ ya heho,
English: Pray that your flight won’t be in the winter.

Kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ matukũ macio gũgaakorwo na mĩnyamaro ĩtarĩ yoneka ĩngĩ tayo kuuma kĩambĩrĩria rĩrĩa Ngai ombire thĩ nginya rĩu, na gũtikoneka ĩngĩ tayo.
English: For in those days there will be oppression, such as there has not been the like from the beginning of the creation which God created until now, and never will be.

Kikuyu: Tiga Mwathani anyihanyiihirie matukũ macio, gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩgatigara. No tondũ wa andũ ake arĩa aamũre, matukũ macio nĩmagakuhĩhio.
English: Unless the Lord had shortened the days, no flesh would have been saved; but for the sake of the chosen ones, whom he picked out, he shortened the days.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ angĩkamwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Kristũ arĩ haha!’ kana amwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Kristũ arĩ haarĩa!’ mũtikanetĩkie ũhoro ũcio.
English: Then if anyone tells you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ don’t believe it.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Kristũ a maheeni na anabii a maheeni nĩmakeyumĩria monanie morirũ na maringe ciama nĩguo mahĩtithie andũ arĩa aamũre, korwo no kũhoteke.
English: For false christs and false prophets will arise and will show signs and wonders, that they may lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, ikaragai mwĩiguĩte tondũ nĩndamwĩra maũndũ mothe mbere ya ihinda rĩtanakinya.
English: But you watch. “Behold, I have told you all things beforehand.

Kikuyu: “No ihinda-inĩ rĩu, thuutha wa maũndũ macio ma mĩnyamaro, “ ‘riũa nĩrĩkagĩa nduma, naguo mweri ndũkaara;
English: But in those days, after that oppression, the sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light,

Kikuyu: njata nacio nĩikaagũa kuuma igũrũ, namo maahinya ma igũrũ nĩmakenyenyio.’
English: the stars will be falling from the sky, and the powers that are in the heavens will be shaken.*

Kikuyu: “Hĩndĩ ĩyo andũ nĩ makoona Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agĩũka arĩ thĩinĩ wa matu, arĩ na hinya mũingĩ mũno o na riiri.
English: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.

Kikuyu: Nake nĩagatũma araika ake macookanĩrĩrie andũ ake arĩa aamũre kuuma mĩena ĩna ĩrĩa yumaga rũhuho, o na kuuma ituri ciothe cia thĩ na cia igũrũ.
English: Then he will send out his angels, and will gather together his chosen ones from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of the sky.

Kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, mwĩrutei na mũkũyũ: Rĩrĩa honge ciaguo ciarĩkia gũthundũra na ciaruta mathangũ- rĩ, nĩmũmenyaga atĩ hĩndĩ ya riũa ĩrĩ hakuhĩ.
English: “Now from the fig tree, learn this parable. When the branch has now become tender and produces its leaves, you know that the summer is near;

Kikuyu: Ũguo noguo, rĩrĩa mũkoona maũndũ macio magĩĩkĩka mũkaamenya ihinda rĩrĩ hakuhĩ, rĩrĩ o mũromo-inĩ.
English: even so you also, when you see these things coming to pass, know that it is near, at the doors.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, rũciaro rũrũ rũtigaathira nginya maũndũ macio mothe makaahingio.
English: Most certainly I say to you, this generation‡ will not pass away until all these things happen.

Kikuyu: Igũrũ na thĩ nĩigathira, no ciugo ciakwa itigathira.
English: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.

Kikuyu: “Gũtirĩ mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ũũĩ ũhoro wa mũthenya ũcio kana ithaa rĩu, o na araika a igũrũ matiũĩ, o na Mũriũ ndooĩ, tiga Ithe wiki!
English: “But of that day or that hour no one knows—not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.

Kikuyu: Ikaragai mwĩiguĩte! Ikaragai mwĩhũgĩte! Mũtiũĩ rĩrĩa ihinda rĩu rĩgaakinya.
English: Watch, keep alert, and pray; for you don’t know when the time is.

Kikuyu: Gũkahaana ta mũndũ ũgũthiĩ rũgendo ũrĩa atigaga egaĩrandungata ciake iroragemũciĩ, o ndungata akamĩhe wĩra wayo, na agatiga atha mũikaria wa mũrango o nake aikarage ehũũgĩte.
English: “It is like a man traveling to another country, having left his house and given authority to his servants, and to each one his work, and also commanded the doorkeeper to keep watch.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ikaragai mwĩiguĩte tondũ mũtiũĩ rĩrĩa mwene mũciĩ agacooka, kana agooka hwaĩ-inĩ, kana ũtukũ gatagatĩ, kana ngũkũ ĩkĩgamba, o na kana gũgĩthererũka.
English: Watch therefore, for you don’t know when the lord of the house is coming—whether at evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning;

Kikuyu: O rĩrĩa angĩũka- rĩ, mũtikanareke amũkore mwĩrigĩtwo.
English: lest, coming suddenly, he might find you sleeping.

Kikuyu: Ũguo ngũmwĩra, noguo ngwĩra andũ othe, atĩrĩ, ‘Ikaragai mwĩiguĩte’.”
English: What I tell you, I tell all: Watch!”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, gwatigaire o mĩthenya ĩĩrĩ nĩguo kũgĩe na Gĩathĩ kĩa Bathaka na kĩa Mĩgate ĩtarĩ Mĩĩkĩre Ndawa ya Kũimbia,* nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na arutani a watho nĩmacaragia ũrĩa mangĩnyiitithia Jesũ na njĩra ya wara nĩguo mamũũrage.
English: It was now two days before the Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread, and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might seize him by deception and kill him.

Kikuyu: Makiuga atĩrĩ, “No tũtingĩmũnyiita hĩndĩ ya Gĩathĩ, tondũ andũ mahota kũruta haaro.”
English: For they said, “Not during the feast, because there might be a riot among the people.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa arĩ itũũra-inĩ rĩa Bethania kwa mũndũ wetagwo Simoni ũrĩa warĩ na mangũ, aikarĩte metha-inĩ akĩrĩa irio-rĩ, nĩhookire mũndũ-wa-nja arĩ na cuba ya maguta ma goro mũno na manungi wega. Akĩũraga cuba ĩyo na akĩmũitĩrĩria maguta macio mũtwe.
English: While he was at Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at the table, a woman came having an alabaster jar of ointment of pure nard—very costly. She broke the jar and poured it over his head.

Kikuyu: Andũ amwe arĩa maarĩ kuo makĩigua ũũru, makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Maguta maya maitangwo ũguo nĩkĩ?
English: But there were some who were indignant among themselves, saying, “Why has this ointment been wasted?

Kikuyu: Maguta macio nĩmangĩendio mbeeca ikĩrĩte mũcara wa mũndũ wa mwaka mũgima nacio mbeeca icio iheo athĩĩni.” Magĩkũũma mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio marĩ na ũũru.
English: For this might have been sold for more than three hundred denarii† and given to the poor.” So they grumbled against her.

Kikuyu: Nowe Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Tiganai nake, mũramũtanga nĩkĩ? Nĩanjĩka ũndũ mwega mũno.
English: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a good work for me.

Kikuyu: Athĩĩni marĩkoragwo na inyuĩ hĩndĩ ciothe na no mũmateithie hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yothe mũngĩenda. No niĩ mũtirĩkoragwo na niĩ hĩndĩ ciothe.
English: For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you want to, you can do them good; but you will not always have me.

Kikuyu: Anjĩka o ũrĩa angĩahota. Aitĩrĩria mwĩrĩ wakwa maguta o mbere nĩ ũndũ wa kũhaarĩria gũthikwo gwakwa.
English: She has done what she could. She has anointed my body beforehand for the burying.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, kũrĩa guothe Ũhoro-ũyũ-Mwega ũkaahunjagio thĩinĩ wa thĩ, ũguo eka nĩũkeeragwo andũ nĩgeetha aririkanagwo.”
English: Most certainly I tell you, wherever this Good News may be preached throughout the whole world, that which this woman has done will also be spoken of for a memorial of her.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Judasi Mũisikariota, ũmwe wa arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ, agĩthiĩ kũrĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene agakunyanĩre Jesũ kũrĩ o.
English: Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went away to the chief priests, that he might deliver him to them.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkena mũno maigua ũguo na makĩmwĩra nĩmekũmũhe mbeeca. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Judasi agĩcaria mweke wa kũmũneana kũrĩ o.
English: They, when they heard it, were glad, and promised to give him money. He sought how he might conveniently deliver him.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya wa mbere wa Gĩathĩ kĩa Mĩgate ĩtarĩ Mĩĩkĩre Ndawa ya Kũimbia, o ũrĩa wathĩnjagwo gatũrũme ka Bathaka-rĩ, arutwo a Jesũ makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũkwenda tũthiĩ kũ tũkahaarĩrie nĩguo ũkarĩĩre irio cia Bathaka kuo?”
English: On the first day of unleavened bread, when they sacrificed the Passover, his disciples asked him, “Where do you want us to go and prepare that you may eat the Passover?”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtũma arutwo ake eerĩ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thiĩi mũtoonye itũũra, na nĩ mũgũcema- nia na mũndũ ũkuuĩte ndigithũ ya maaĩ. Mũrũmĩrĩrei.
English: He sent two of his disciples and said to them, “Go into the city, and there a man carrying a pitcher of water will meet you. Follow him,

Kikuyu: Kũrĩa egũtoonya mwĩre mwene nyũmba ĩyo atĩrĩ, ‘Mũrutani arooria atĩrĩ, nyũmba yakwa ya ageni ĩrĩ ha, harĩa ingĩrĩĩra irio cia Bathaka na arutwo akwa?’
English: and wherever he enters in, tell the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says, “Where is the guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?” ’

Kikuyu: Nake nĩekũmuonia nyũmba nene ya igũrũ ĩkĩrĩtwo indo na ĩgathondekwo wega. Haarĩriai irio cia Bathaka iitũ kuo.”
English: He will himself show you a large upper room furnished and ready. Get ready for us there.”

Kikuyu: Arutwo acio makiumagara, magĩtoonya itũũra, magĩkora maũndũ mahaana o ta ũrĩa Jesũ aamerĩte. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩhaarĩria irio cia Bathaka.
English: His disciples went out, and came into the city, and found things as he had said to them, and they prepared the Passover.

Kikuyu: Na gwakinya hwaĩ-inĩ, Jesũ agĩkinya kuo marĩ na arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ.
English: When it was evening he came with the twelve.

Kikuyu: Maikarĩte metha- inĩ makĩrĩa akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũmwe wanyu nĩekũngunyanĩra, ũmwe wa arĩa mararĩĩanĩra na niĩ.”
English: As they sat and were eating, Jesus said, “Most certainly I tell you, one of you will betray me—he who eats with me.”

Kikuyu: Makĩnyiitwo nĩ kĩeha mũno, na o ũmwe akamũũria atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ hihi nĩ niĩ?”
English: They began to be sorrowful, and to ask him one by one, “Surely not I?” And another said, “Surely not I?”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũmwe wanyu inyuĩ ikũmi na eerĩ, ũrĩa ũgũtobokanĩria mũgate mbakũri-inĩ hamwe na niĩ.
English: He answered them, “It is one of the twelve, he who dips with me in the dish.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wa Mũndũ egũthiĩ o ta ũrĩa ũhoro wake wandĩkĩtwo. No mũndũ ũrĩa ũgaakunyanĩra Mũrũ wa Mũndũ kaĩ arĩ na haaro-ĩ! Nĩ kaba mũndũ ũcio atangĩaciarirwo.”
English: For the Son of Man goes as it is written about him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would be better for that man if he had not been born.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ o makĩrĩĩaga-rĩ, Jesũ akĩoya mũgate, agĩcookia ngaatho, agĩcooka akĩwenyũranga, akĩhe arutwo ake, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Oyai; ũyũ nĩguo mwĩrĩ wakwa.”
English: As they were eating, Jesus took bread, and when he had blessed it, he broke it and gave to them, and said, “Take, eat. This is my body.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩoya gĩkombe, agĩcookia ngaatho akĩmanengera, nao othe magĩkĩnyuĩra.
English: He took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave to them. They all drank of it.

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ĩno nĩyo thakame yakwa ya kĩrĩkanĩro, ĩrĩa ĩitĩtwo nĩ ũndũ wa andũ aingĩ.
English: He said to them, “This is my blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ndikũnyua rĩngĩ ndibei ya maciaro ma mũthabibũ, nginya mũthenya ũrĩa ngaamĩnyua ĩrĩ njerũ ũthamaki-inĩ wa Ngai.”
English: Most certainly I tell you, I will no more drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it anew in God’s Kingdom.”

Kikuyu: Na maarĩkia kũina rwĩmbo, makiumagara magĩthiĩ kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩa Mĩtamaiyũ.
English: When they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ inyuothe nĩmũrĩndirika, nĩgũkorwo nĩ kwandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “ ‘Nĩngaringa mũrĩithi, nacio ngʼondu ihurunjũke.’
English: Jesus said to them, “All of you will be made to stumble because of me tonight, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’*

Kikuyu: No ndaariũka, nĩngamũtongoria gũthiĩ Galili.”
English: However, after I am raised up, I will go before you into Galilee.”

Kikuyu: Petero akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “O na othe mangĩgũtirika, niĩ ndingĩgũtirika.”
English: But Peter said to him, “Although all will be offended, yet I will not.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũmũthĩ, o ũtukũ ũyũ, ngũkũ ĩtanakũga keerĩ, nĩũrĩngaana maita matatũ.”
English: Jesus said to him, “Most certainly I tell you that you today, even this night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.”

Kikuyu: No Petero akĩaria ahinyĩrĩirie, akiuga atĩrĩ, “O na angĩkorwo ndĩrĩkuanĩra nawe, niĩ nd- ingĩgũkaana!” O na acio angĩ othe makiuga o ũguo.
English: But he spoke all the more, “If I must die with you, I will not deny you.” They all said the same thing.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩkinya handũ heetagwo Gethisemane, nake Jesũ akĩĩra arutwo ake atĩrĩ, “Ikarai haha thiĩ ngahooe.”
English: They came to a place which was named Gethsemane. He said to his disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”

Kikuyu: Akĩoya Petero na Jakubu na Johana agĩthiĩ nao, na o hĩndĩ ĩyo akĩnyiitwo nĩ kĩeha kĩnene na agĩthĩĩnĩka mũno.
English: He took with him Peter, James, and John, and began to be greatly troubled and distressed.

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngoro yakwa nĩĩraigua kĩeha mũno o ta ingĩkua, ikarai haha na mwĩhũge.”
English: He said to them, “My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here and watch.”

Kikuyu: Na aathiathia hanini, akĩĩgũithia thĩ, akĩhooya atĩ korwo kwahoteka, ihinda rĩu rĩmweherere.
English: He went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that if it were possible, the hour might pass away from him.

Kikuyu: Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Abba, Baba, maũndũ mothe nĩmahotekaga nĩwe. Njehereria gĩkombe gĩkĩ. No ti ũrĩa ngwenda niĩ, no nĩ ũrĩa wee ũkwenda.”
English: He said, “Abba,‡ Father, all things are possible to you. Please remove this cup from me. However, not what I desire, but what you desire.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ agĩcooka harĩ arutwo ake akĩmakora makomete. Akĩũria Petero atĩrĩ, “Simoni, kaĩ ũkomete? Ndũngĩhota gũikara ithaa rĩmwe wĩiguĩte?
English: He came and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, “Simon, are you sleeping? Couldn’t you watch one hour?

Kikuyu: Mwĩhũgagei na mũhooyage nĩgeetha mũtikagwe magerio-inĩ. Ngoro nĩyenda, no mwĩrĩ ndũrĩ na hinya.”
English: Watch and pray, that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”

Kikuyu: O rĩngĩ agĩthiĩ akĩhooya o ũguo.
English: Again he went away and prayed, saying the same words.

Kikuyu: Na acooka rĩngĩ harĩo, agĩkora makomete tondũ nĩmahĩtĩtwo nĩ toro. Nao makĩrigwo nĩ ũrĩa mangĩmwĩra.
English: Again he returned and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy; and they didn’t know what to answer him.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ agĩũka ihinda rĩa gatatũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “O na rĩu mũrĩ o toro, o mũhurũkĩte? Kinyiai hau! Ithaa nĩrĩakinya. Atĩrĩrĩ, Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩakunyanĩirwo akaneanwo moko-inĩ ma andũ ehia.
English: He came the third time and said to them, “Sleep on now, and take your rest. It is enough. The hour has come. Behold, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

Kikuyu: Ũkĩrai, tũthiĩ! Ũrĩa ũkũngunyanĩra nĩarooka.”
English: Arise! Let’s get going. Behold, he who betrays me is at hand.”

Kikuyu: O akĩaragia-rĩ, Judasi, ũmwe wa arutwo arĩa ikũmi na eerĩ, agĩkinya. Ookire arĩ na gĩkundi kĩnene kĩa andũ marĩ na hiũ cia njora na njũgũma, matũmĩtwo nĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho na athuuri.
English: Immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, came—and with him a multitude with swords and clubs, from the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders.

Kikuyu: Judasi ũcio wamũkunyanĩire nĩamaheete kĩmenyithia akameera atĩrĩ, “Ũrĩa ndĩmumunya nĩwe; mũmũnyiite na mũmũtware arangĩirwo.”
English: Now he who betrayed him had given them a sign, saying, “Whomever I will kiss, that is he. Seize him, and lead him away safely.”

Kikuyu: Judasi o rĩmwe agĩthiĩ harĩ Jesũ, akĩmwĩta atĩrĩ, “Rabii!” na akĩmũmumunya.
English: When he had come, immediately he came to him and said, “Rabbi! Rabbi!” and kissed him.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ acio makĩguthũkĩra Jesũ makĩmũnyiita.
English: They laid their hands on him and seized him.

Kikuyu: Na ũmwe wa arĩa maarũngiĩ hakuhĩ agĩcomora rũhiũ rwake rwa njora agĩtinia gũtũ kwa ndungata ya mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene.
English: But a certain one of those who stood by drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ ndĩ mũtongoria wa akararia a watho atĩ nĩkĩo muoka kũũnyiita mũrĩ na hiũ cia njora na njũgũma?
English: Jesus answered them, “Have you come out, as against a robber, with swords and clubs to seize me?

Kikuyu: Ndĩratũũraga na inyuĩ hekarũ-inĩ ngĩrutanaga mũthenya o mũthenya na mũtingĩĩnyiita. No rĩrĩ, Maandĩko no nginya mahiinge.”
English: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and you didn’t arrest me. But this is so that the Scriptures might be fulfilled.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo arutwo ake othe makĩmũtiganĩria, makĩũra.
English: They all left him, and fled.

Kikuyu: Na mwanake ũmwe ũteehumbĩte kĩndũ tiga taama wa gatani akĩrũmĩrĩra Jesũ. Na rĩrĩa maamũnyiitire,
English: A certain young man followed him, having a linen cloth thrown around himself over his naked body. The young men grabbed him,

Kikuyu: agĩtiga taama wake nao, akĩũra e njaga.
English: but he left the linen cloth and fled from them naked.

Kikuyu: Nao andũ acio magĩtwara Jesũ kũrĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene, nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene othe, na athuuri na arutani a watho makĩgomana hamwe.
English: They led Jesus away to the high priest. All the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes came together with him.

Kikuyu: Petero akĩmũrũmĩrĩra arĩ haraaya o nginya agĩkinya nja ya mũciĩ wa mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio mũnene. Agĩikarania na thigari kũu agĩota mwaki.
English: Peter had followed him from a distance, until he came into the court of the high priest. He was sitting with the officers, and warming himself in the light of the fire.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, o na Kĩama kĩu gĩothe,† magĩcaria ũira wa kũũragithia Jesũ, no mationire.
English: Now the chief priests and the whole council sought witnesses against Jesus to put him to death, and found none.

Kikuyu: Na andũ aingĩ nĩmarutire ũira wa maheeni, no ũira wao ũkĩaga kũiguana.
English: For many gave false testimony against him, and their testimony didn’t agree with each other.

Kikuyu: Gũgĩcooka gũkĩrũgama andũ amwe, nao makĩruta ũira wa maheeni makiuga atĩrĩ,
English: Some stood up and gave false testimony against him, saying,

Kikuyu: “Nĩtwaiguire akiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩngatharia hekarũ ĩno yakĩtwo nĩ andũ, na njake ĩngĩ ĩtakĩtwo nĩ mũndũ na ihinda rĩa mĩthenya ĩtatũ.’ ”
English: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another made without hands.’ ”

Kikuyu: No o na moiga ũguo, ũira wao ũkĩaga kũiguana.
English: Even so, their testimony didn’t agree.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene akĩrũgama gatagatĩ kao, akĩũria Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ wee ũtarĩ na ũndũ ũngĩcookia? Nĩ ũhoro ũrĩkũ ũyũ ũrathitangwo naguo?”
English: The high priest stood up in the middle, and asked Jesus, “Have you no answer? What is it which these testify against you?”

Kikuyu: No Jesũ agĩkira ki, na ndacookie ũndũ. O rĩngĩ mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio mũnene akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Kristũ, Mũrũ wa Ũrĩa Mũrathime?”
English: But he stayed quiet, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ nĩ niĩ. Na nĩmũkona Mũrũ wa Mũndũ aikarĩte guoko-inĩ kwa ũrĩo kwa Ũrĩa-ũrĩ-Hinya, na agĩũka arĩ igũrũ rĩa matu.”
English: Jesus said, “I am. You will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power, and coming with the clouds of the sky.”

Kikuyu: Mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũcio mũnene agĩtembũranga nguo ciake. Akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Tũgũkĩbatario nĩ aira angĩ a kĩ?
English: The high priest tore his clothes and said, “What further need have we of witnesses?

Kikuyu: Nĩmweiguĩra akĩruma Ngai. Mũgwĩciiria atĩa?” Nao othe makĩmũtuĩra atĩ aagĩrĩirwo nĩ kũũragwo.
English: You have heard the blasphemy! What do you think?” They all condemned him to be worthy of death.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo amwe makĩambĩrĩria kũmũtuĩra mata, na makĩmũhumbĩra ũthiũ, makĩmũringa na ngundi makĩmwĩraga atĩrĩ, “Ta kĩrathe ũhoro.” Nacio thigari ikĩmuoya, ikĩmũhũũra.
English: Some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to beat him with fists, and to tell him, “Prophesy!” The officers struck him with the palms of their hands.

Kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩyo Petero aikarĩte o hau nja-rĩ, ũmwe wa ndungata cia airĩtu cia mũthĩnjĩri-Ngai ũrĩa mũnene akĩgerera ho.
English: As Peter was in the courtyard below, one of the maids of the high priest came,

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa oonire Petero agĩota mwaki, akĩmũrora wega. “Akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘O nawe uuma hamwe na Jesũ ũcio wa Nazarethi’.”
English: and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, “You were also with the Nazarene, Jesus!”

Kikuyu: Nowe Petero agĩkaana ũhoro ũcio, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndiũĩ ũhoro ũcio, o na ndiramenya nĩ atĩa ũroiga,” na akiumagara agĩthiĩ itoonyero-inĩ.
English: But he denied it, saying, “I neither know nor understand what you are saying.” He went out on the porch, and the rooster crowed.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa ndungata ĩyo ya mũirĩtu yamuonire ho, o rĩngĩ ĩkĩĩra arĩa maarũngiĩ ho atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ nĩ ũmwe wao.”
English: The maid saw him and began again to tell those who stood by, “This is one of them.”

Kikuyu: No Petero agĩkaana ũhoro ũcio rĩngĩ. Thuutha wa kahinda kanini, arĩa maarũgamĩte hakuhĩ makĩĩra Petero atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ ũrĩ ũmwe wao, nĩ ũndũ wĩ Mũgalili.”‡
English: But he again denied it. After a little while again those who stood by said to Peter, “You truly are one of them, for you are a Galilean, and your speech shows it.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩambĩrĩria kwĩhĩta akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndiũĩ mũndũ ũyũ mũraaria ũhoro wake.”
English: But he began to curse and to swear, “I don’t know this man of whom you speak!”

Kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo ngũkũ ĩgĩkũga hĩndĩ ya keerĩ. Nake Petero akĩririkana ũhoro ũrĩa Jesũ aamwĩrĩte atĩrĩ, “Ngũkũ ĩtanakũga keerĩ nĩũrĩngaana maita matatũ.” Nake akĩambĩrĩria kũrĩra.
English: The rooster crowed the second time. Peter remembered the words that Jesus said to him, “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” When he thought about that, he wept.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, rũciinĩ tene, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene na athuuri, na arutani a watho, na Kĩama gĩothe magĩtua ũrĩa megwĩka. Makĩoha Jesũ, makĩmũtwara, makĩmũneana kũrĩ Pilato.
English: Immediately in the morning the chief priests, with the elders, scribes, and the whole council, held a consultation, bound Jesus, carried him away, and delivered him up to Pilate.

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Mũthamaki wa Ayahudi?” Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ, ũguo woiga nĩguo.”
English: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered, “So you say.”

Kikuyu: Nao athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene makĩmũthitangĩra maũndũ maingĩ.
English: The chief priests accused him of many things.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Pilato akĩmũũria o rĩngĩ atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ wee ũtarĩ na ũndũ ũgũcookia? Ta rora maũndũ marĩa mothe maragũthitangĩra.”
English: Pilate again asked him, “Have you no answer? See how many things they testify against you!”

Kikuyu: No Jesũ ndaigana gũcookia ũndũ, nake Pilato akĩgega.
English: But Jesus made no further answer, so that Pilate marveled.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, kwarĩ mũtugo hĩndĩ ya Iruga rĩu rĩa Bathaka, Pilato nĩamohoragĩra mwohwo ũmwe, o ũrĩa andũ mangĩetirie.
English: Now at the feast he used to release to them one prisoner, whomever they asked of him.

Kikuyu: Na nĩ kwarĩ mũndũ ũmwe wetagwo Baraba woohetwo hamwe na arĩa maambĩrĩirie ngũĩ ya gũũkĩrĩra thirikari na makooraga andũ thĩinĩ wa mbũkĩrĩra ĩyo.
English: There was one called Barabbas, bound with his fellow insurgents, men who in the insurrection had committed murder.

Kikuyu: Nakĩo kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene gĩgĩthiĩ harĩ Pilato, gĩkĩmũũria eeke o ũrĩa aamenyerete gwĩka.
English: The multitude, crying aloud, began to ask him to do as he always did for them.

Kikuyu: Pilato akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ mũkwenda ndĩmuohorere mũthamaki wa Ayahudi?”
English: Pilate answered them, saying, “Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo nĩamenyaga atĩ athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene maaneanĩte Jesũ kũrĩ we nĩ ũndũ wa ũiru.
English: For he perceived that for envy the chief priests had delivered him up.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene makĩringĩrĩria kĩrĩndĩ kĩu kĩĩre Pilato ohore Baraba handũ ha Jesũ.
English: But the chief priests stirred up the multitude, that he should release Barabbas to them instead.

Kikuyu: Pilato akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Mũgũkĩenda njĩke atĩa na mũndũ ũyũ mwĩtaga mũthamaki wa Ayahudi?”
English: Pilate again asked them, “What then should I do to him whom you call the King of the Jews?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩanĩrĩra, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwambe mũtĩ igũrũ!”
English: They cried out again, “Crucify him!”

Kikuyu: Nake Pilato akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩkĩ? Nĩ ngero ĩrĩkũ agerete?” No-o magĩkĩrĩrĩria kwanĩrĩra, makiugaga atĩrĩ, “Mwambe mũtĩ igũrũ!”
English: Pilate said to them, “Why, what evil has he done?” But they cried out exceedingly, “Crucify him!”

Kikuyu: Na tondũ nĩendaga gũkenia kĩrĩndĩ kĩu, Pilato akĩmohorera Baraba. Akĩhũũrithia Jesũ iboko, agĩcooka akĩmũneana akambwo mũtĩ igũrũ.
English: Pilate, wishing to please the multitude, released Barabbas to them, and handed over Jesus, when he had flogged him, to be crucified.

Kikuyu: Nacio thigari igĩtwara Jesũ gĩikaro-inĩ kĩa barũthi (na nĩkĩo gĩetagwo Puratoria); igĩcookanĩrĩria mbũtũ yothe ya thigari.
English: The soldiers led him away within the court, which is the Praetorium; and they called together the whole cohort.

Kikuyu: Ikĩmũhumba nguo ndaaya ya rangi wa ndathi, igĩcooka igĩtuma thũmbĩ ya mĩigua, ikĩmwĩkĩra mũtwe.
English: They clothed him with purple; and weaving a crown of thorns, they put it on him.

Kikuyu: Ikĩambĩrĩria kũmũnyũrũria, ikĩmwĩraga atĩrĩ, “Ũkĩrĩ mũhoro, wee mũthamaki wa Ayahudi?”
English: They began to salute him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”

Kikuyu: Ikĩmũhũũra na rũthanju mũtwe maita maingĩ ikĩmũtuagĩra mata. Ikĩĩgũithia thĩ mbere yake, ikĩmũturagĩria ndu ta ikũmũhooya.
English: They struck his head with a reed and spat on him, and bowing their knees, did homage to him.

Kikuyu: Na ciarĩkia kũmũnyũrũria- rĩ, ikĩmũruta nguo ĩyo ndaaya ya rangi wa ndathi, ikĩmũhumba nguo ciake mwene. Igĩcooka ikĩmuumagaria ikamwambe mũtĩ igũrũ.
English: When they had mocked him, they took the purple cloak off him, and put his own garments on him. They led him out to crucify him.

Kikuyu: Na marĩ njĩra thigari ikĩnyiita mũndũ wahĩtũkaga akiuma mĩgũnda-inĩ, wetagwo Simoni wa kuuma Kurene, ithe wa Alekisandero na Rufusi, na ikĩmũkuuithia mũtharaba wa Jesũ na hinya.
English: They compelled one passing by, coming from the country, Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to go with them that he might bear his cross.

Kikuyu: Igĩgĩkinyia Jesũ handũ harĩa heetagwo Gologotha (ũguo nĩ kuuga Handũ hahaana ta Ihĩndĩ rĩa Mũtwe).
English: They brought him to the place called Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, “The place of a skull.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ igĩcooka ikĩmũhe ndibei ĩtukanĩtio na manemane, nake Jesũ akĩrega kũmĩnyua.
English: They offered him wine mixed with myrrh to drink, but he didn’t take it.

Kikuyu: Igĩkĩmwamba mũtĩ igũrũ. Ikĩgayana nguo ciake, na ũndũ wa gũcicuukĩra mĩtĩ nĩguo imenye o ĩrĩa mũndũ egũkuua.
English: Crucifying him, they parted his garments among them, casting lots on them, what each should take.

Kikuyu: Kwarĩ thaa ithatũ cia rũciinĩ rĩrĩa ciamwambire mũtĩ igũrũ.
English: It was the third hour† when they crucified him.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũrĩa aathitangĩirwo wandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩwe MŨTHAMAKI WA AYAHUDI.”
English: The superscription of his accusation was written over him: “THE KING OF THE JEWS.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ ikĩamba atunyani eerĩ hamwe nake, ũmwe mwena wake wa ũrĩo, nake ũcio ũngĩ mwena wake wa ũmotho.
English: With him they crucified two robbers, one on his right hand, and one on his left.

Kikuyu: (Namo Maandĩko makĩhingio marĩa moigaga atĩrĩ, “Aataranĩirio na andũ arĩa aagarari watho.”)
English: The Scripture was fulfilled which says, “He was counted with transgressors.”‡

Kikuyu: Andũ arĩa maahĩtũkagĩra hau nĩmamũrumaga makĩinagia mĩtwe yao, makoiga atĩrĩ, “Hĩ! Wee ũngĩratharirie hekarũ na ũmĩake na mĩthenya ĩtatũ-rĩ,
English: Those who passed by blasphemed him, wagging their heads and saying, “Ha! You who destroy the temple and build it in three days,

Kikuyu: harũrũka uume mũtharaba-inĩ ũcio, wĩhonokie!”
English: save yourself, and come down from the cross!”

Kikuyu: O ũndũ ũmwe athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho nĩmamũnyũrũragia, makerana atĩrĩ, “Nĩarahonokagia arĩa angĩ, no ndangĩhota kwĩhonokia we mwene.
English: Likewise, also the chief priests mocking among themselves with the scribes said, “He saved others. He can’t save himself.

Kikuyu: Kristũ ũyũ, Mũthamaki wa Isiraeli-rĩ, ta nĩakĩharũrũke rĩu oime mũtharaba-inĩ, nĩguo tuone twĩtĩkie.” O nao arĩa maambanĩirio hamwe nake, makĩmũruma.
English: Let the Christ, the King of Israel, now come down from the cross, that we may see and believe him.”§ Those who were crucified with him also insulted him.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, thaa thita cia mũthenya ciakinya, bũrũri wothe ũkĩgĩa nduma nginya thaa kenda.
English: When the sixth hour† had come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.‡

Kikuyu: Na thaa kenda Jesũ akĩgũthũka na mũgambo mũnene, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Eloi, Eloi, lama sabakithani?” naguo ũguo nĩ kuuga atĩrĩ, “Ngai wakwa, Ngai wakwa, wandiganĩria nĩkĩ?”
